groups.group_label: Complete 30 semester units from the following

notes: Guarantee your admission to UC Davis! The UC Davis Transfer Admission Guarantee (TAG) is a written agreement ensuring your admission to UC Davis from any California Community College. Please visit the TAG webpage for more information!

Transfer students are strongly encouraged to work closely with the California Community College counselors for transfer preparation to UC Davis. Transfer students may also contact a UC Davis transfer advisor for assistance. The below agreements should be utilized in conjunction with professional advising.
Please note that the Department Agreements as published on ASSIST are also important documents and should be referenced in conjunction with the major agreements as needed.

UC Davis graduates have learned that a major in Japanese is a meaningful, earned distinction that facilitates entrance to graduate programs and professional schools. In addition, job opportunities abound in virtually all career paths, especially for graduates who have completed study abroad.

The department offers a core language program in Japanese , and courses in literature and culture. Practical language skills are taught using contemporary methods and materials so that upon entering the upper division a student will have attained substantial fluency in the spoken language (listening and speaking) and the written language (reading and writing). Upper division courses balance the need to further develop language skills with the need to understand and appreciate the richness and diversity of Japanese culture. All students are encouraged to combine their study of language and literature with courses in related fields, and to study abroad through the Education Abroad Program, the UC Davis Study Abroad Program or through internships in Japan.
Please refer to the Transfer Admissions Requirements for information on admissions to this major.

Japanese Placement Examination Note: Course placement for Japanese language courses is strictly by proficiency exam. See information related to Japanese proficiency exams and also information regarding foreign language proficiency exams. For more information, contact the Japanese department academic advisor or visit the Japanese Placement Exam website.
